text: A bleeding occurred during the dissection of segmental artery 9.
After initial unsuccessful bleeding control by compression, multiple clips were applied.
A tourniquet was inserted for additional local bleeding control.
The lesion was then treated using a monofilament suture.
Due to bleeding control, the clips and subsequently the tonic could be removed again.
Segmental artery 10 could then be detached.
The entire procedure was completed minimally invasively and segment 10 was successfully resected.
Pathologically, NSCVC PT1B R0 was confirmed and the patient was discharged in stable condition after 8 days.
